title: Git Staging & Commits
description: Learn to stage specific files, unstage changes, inspect diffs, amend commits, and write effective commit messages.

tasks.prompt: Stage only the file 'app.py' for the next commit.
tasks.hint: Use git add followed by the filename.
tasks.explanation: git add stages specific file changes without affecting other files.

tasks.prompt: Interactively stage hunks of changes (patch mode).
tasks.hint: Use git add with the -p flag.
tasks.explanation: git add -p lets you selectively stage portions of modified files.

tasks.prompt: Unstage 'app.py' while keeping its working directory changes.
tasks.hint: Use git reset HEAD with the filename.
tasks.explanation: git reset HEAD <file> removes a file from the staging area.

tasks.prompt: Show unstaged changes in the working directory.
tasks.hint: Use git diff with no arguments.
tasks.explanation: git diff compares working tree changes against the staging area.

tasks.prompt: Show changes that are currently staged for commit.
tasks.explanation: git diff --staged previews exactly what will be committed.

tasks.prompt: Commit staged changes with message 'fix login bug' and show the diffstat.
tasks.hint: Use git commit -v -m with your message.
tasks.explanation: The -v flag displays the staged diff in the commit editor/output.

tasks.prompt: Amend the previous commit without changing its message.
tasks.explanation: git commit --amend --no-edit adds staged changes to the last commit.

tasks.prompt: Show repository status in short format.
tasks.explanation: git status -s provides a compact view of staged and unstaged files.
